story: Choo-choo! The whistle blew as India's first passenger train chugged to life on April 16, 1853. This historic journey connected Bori Bunder in Bombay (now Mumbai) to Thane, marking the birth of India's vast railway network. Excited passengers climbed aboard, ready to experience a new way of traveling across their country.
activity_question: What two cities did India's first passenger train connect?
bonus_fact: The train's route was about as long as 340 football fields placed end-to-end!

title: Shaken City: San Salvador's Earthquake
story: The ground rumbled and buildings crumbled as a powerful earthquake struck San Salvador in 1854. On April 16, the capital city of El Salvador faced destruction, but its people showed incredible strength. They banded together, helping neighbors and starting the long process of rebuilding their beloved home.

story: Gunshots rang out in Dodge City, Kansas! On April 16, 1881, famous lawman Bat Masterson faced his last gun battle. story: Colditz Castle stood as a fortress for captured soldiers in World War II. On April 16, 1945, American troops stormed the castle in Germany, freeing the prisoners inside. title: Freedom Rings in Groningen
story: Cheers erupted in Groningen, Netherlands as German troops laid down their arms. On April 16, 1945, Canadian and Dutch forces accepted the surrender, marking a crucial victory in World War II. title: Western US Faces Decades-Long Dry Spell
story: Thirsty trees and parched rivers signal trouble. On April 16, 2020, scientists sounded the alarm. A new study in the journal Science revealed a shocking truth: the western United States was already in the grip of a megadrought. This wasn't just a short dry spell, but a massive water shortage that could last for decades.

It was experiencing a heat wave
activity_answer: It was in the grip of a megadrought

title: Martin Luther's Bold Stand
story: Imagine facing powerful leaders to defend your beliefs! In 1521, Martin Luther bravely traveled to an important meeting called the Diet of Worms.
